author: Augustine of Hippo
book: Against the Epistle of Manichæus, Called Fundamental.

classification.cautionReason: This work discusses heresy in order to refute it; do not classify it as heretical merely from its title.

lineage: Writings in Connection with the Manichæan Controversy.
Against the Epistle of Manichæus, Called Fundamental.
The Form of the Region of Light the Worse of the Two.

ccel.title: NPNF1-04. Augustine: The Writings Against the Manichaeans and Against the Donatists

source.title: NPNF1-04. Augustine: The Writings Against the Manichaeans and Against the Donatists

title: The Form of the Region of Light the Worse of the Two.

source.title: NPNF1-04. Augustine: The Writings Against the Manichaeans and Against the Donatists

verses.book: Against the Epistle of Manichæus, Called Fundamental.
verses.text: Well, then, if this is so, the region of darkness is clearly touched on two sides by the region of light.

verses.book: Against the Epistle of Manichæus, Called Fundamental.
verses.text: And if it is touched on two sides, it must touch on two.

verses.book: Against the Epistle of Manichæus, Called Fundamental.
verses.text: So much for its being on one side, as we were told before.

verses.book: Against the Epistle of Manichæus, Called Fundamental.
verses.text: And what an unseemly appearance is this of the region of light!—like a cloven arch, with a black wedge inserted below, bounded only in the direction of the cleft, and having a void space interposed where the boundless emptiness stretches above the region of darkness.

verses.book: Against the Epistle of Manichæus, Called Fundamental.
verses.text: Indeed, the form of the region of darkness is better than that of the region of light: for the former cleaves, the latter is cloven; the former fills the gap which is made in the latter; the former has no void in it, while the latter is undefined in all directions, except that where it is filled up by the wedge of darkness.

verses.book: Against the Epistle of Manichæus, Called Fundamental.
verses.text: In an ignorant and greedy notion of giving more honor to a number of pans than to a single one, so that the region of light should have six, three upwards and three downwards, they have made this region be split up, instead of sundering the other.

verses.book: Against the Epistle of Manichæus, Called Fundamental.
verses.text: For, according to this figure, though there may be no commixture of darkness with light, there is certainly penetration.
